_comment: Phase 1 / Item #1 of IMPLEMENTATION_ROADMAP.md. See sources/_schema.md.

sources.label: NIH Office of Dietary Supplements
sources.notes: Health-professional fact sheets are the primary product. Stable URL pattern: ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/{Slug}-HealthProfessional/. Covers ~80 supplements. Updated 1-3x/year per fact sheet.

sources.label: European Food Safety Authority
sources.notes: Scientific opinions PDF dossiers. Search-result extraction is live; PDF dossier text extraction (UL values, conclusions sections) is a future deepening. Hard upper-limit values are particularly valuable for Tier 4 / safety entries.

sources.label: European Medicines Agency — HMPC monographs
sources.notes: Herbal Medicinal Products Committee monographs cover ~150 herbal supplements with EU regulatory positions. Search-result extraction is live; per-monograph PDF section extraction (Therapeutic Indications, Contraindications) is a future deepening.

sources.label: Cochrane Library
sources.notes: Abstracts and Plain Language Summaries are free and usually sufficient for tier calls. Full-text reviews require subscription. The CRSO endpoint accepts free-text searches and returns structured XML.

sources.label: openFDA Drug Adverse Events (FAERS)
sources.notes: Real-world adverse event reports. Free REST API, no auth. Particularly useful for Tier 4 risk content and for spotting emerging safety signals.

sources.label: openFDA Drug Labels (SPLs)
sources.notes: Phase 2 / Item #2: mines FDA drug labels' drug_interactions sections for supplement mentions. Output is candidate drug-supplement pairs — human review before adding to DRUG_INTERACTIONS.pairs in app.js. Different from openfda (FAERS adverse-event API).

sources.label: DrugBank
sources.notes: Phase 2 follow-up: pending academic license application. See docs/drugbank-integration.md for the application process and the integration plan. Most curated drug-interaction dataset available — meaningfully more accurate than openFDA SPL mining.

sources.label: Health Canada NNHPD monographs
sources.notes: ~600 structured monographs with approved doses, claims, and contraindications. Bulk-downloadable. One-time ingestion + nightly cache refresh.

sources.label: WHO monographs on selected medicinal plants
sources.notes: 4 published volumes covering ~120 plants. PDFs only — manual extraction. Valuable for traditional-use claims that need a global authority for context.

sources.label: NIH MedlinePlus / NCCIH
sources.notes: Consumer-facing redundancy check — useful as a sanity check on tier calls and on dose recommendations for general public. Lower priority than the Tier 1/2 sources.
